description_long: This object is a classic shotgun with a full-length wooden stock and fore-end, complemented by a metallic barrel and receiver. The stock and fore-end are finished in a dark, polished wood, while the barrel and receiver are metallic gray, giving the shotgun a traditional and robust appearance. The barrel is long and cylindrical, with a front sight and a tubular magazine running beneath it. The trigger guard is rounded and metallic, enclosing a single trigger, and the action mechanism appears to be lever-operated, with a visible lever beneath the receiver. The overall design is utilitarian, with minimal ornamentation, emphasizing function over form. This shotgun would be commonly found in urban environments such as police armories, private residences, or security settings.
description: A traditional shotgun with a polished wooden stock and fore-end, metallic barrel, and a lever-action mechanism. The design is robust and functional, with a long barrel and rounded trigger guard.
description_view_0: Front view showing the circular muzzle of the barrel and the alignment of the tubular magazine beneath it.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the full length of the shotgun, including the wooden stock, fore-end, metallic receiver, and lever-action mechanism.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the butt of the stock and the narrow profile of the barrel and receiver.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the same features as view 1, with clear visibility of the lever and trigger guard.
attribute_barrel_configuration: single-barrel, long cylindrical barrel with a tubular magazine beneath
attribute_trigger_guard_design: rounded metallic trigger guard of medium size, enclosing a single trigger, with no visible finger grooves or reinforcement
